digest.component_or_area: rebar MCP server (rebar-mcp) HTTP transport + authentication framework
propositions: rebar-mcp is stdio-only with no HTTP transport and no authentication, so a shared/hosted store cannot be reached by a remote MCP client without exposing it unauthenticated.

Add an optional Streamable-HTTP transport where rebar acts as an OAuth 2.1 Resource Server that validates inbound bearer tokens and advertises RFC 9728 Protected Resource Metadata, delegating identity to the deploying org's IdP.

A composite TokenVerifier serves as the single audience/fail-closed choke point, rejecting any token unless AccessToken.resource is present and equals the single configured auth_resource_server_url (RFC 8707), since the SDK does not validate audience.
Ship five selectable verifier modes (static bearer, JWKS/JWT, RFC 7662 introspection, trusted-proxy passthrough, pluggable custom) configured through flat mcp.auth_* keys and REBAR_MCP_* env vars.

Enforce fail-closed startup and downgrade: unknown strategy, empty composite with auth enabled, auth-enabled HTTP with no verifier, and unauthenticated HTTP without the explicit ack all refuse to start; non-loopback bind requires allowlists plus a TLS-at-edge acknowledgment.
Behavior must remain purely additive: stdio stays the default, auth is off by default, and existing stdio deployments and MCP tests are unaffected when new keys are unset.
